'''Jacob Miller''' (born 22 August 1992) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|so}} or {{rlp|sh}} for the [[Mullumbimby Giants]] in the [[Northern Rivers Regional Rugby League]] in [[New South Wales]].

He previously played for the [[Wests Tigers]] in the [[NRL]], and for [[Hull F.C.]] and [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]]. He has also spent time on loan at [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. He has played at representative level for the [[Combined Nationalities rugby league team|Combined Nations All Stars]].

==Background==
Miller was born in [[Sydney]], New South Wales, Australia.

Hailing from [[Ocean Shores, New South Wales|Ocean Shores]],<ref>{{cite web|title=Wests Tigers sign Mullumbimby's Jacob Miller|url=http://www.rlcm.com.au/latest-news/8876-rugby-league|author=Mitchell Craig|access-date=29 June 2011|website=rlcm.com.au|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111001001635/http://www.rlcm.com.au/latest-news/8876-rugby-league|archive-date=1 October 2011|url-status=dead}}</ref> Miller played junior football with [[Mullumbimby Giants]], winning a premiership with the Giants in the under 18s competition in 2007. He was a pupil at Mullumbimby High School before he moved to Sydney and was a pupil at [[Matraville Sports High School]] when he was chosen as halfback for the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys]] in 2009.<ref>{{cite news|publisher=Central Western Daily|title=Canowindra star makes Australian Schoolboys|author=Daniel Briggs|url=http://www.centralwesterndaily.com.au/news/local/sport/rugby-league/canowindra-star-makes-australian-schoolboys/1574386.aspx?storypage=0|access-date=29 June 2011|date=22 July 2009}}</ref>

On a scholarship with the [[Sydney Roosters]] from age 14 onwards, Miller was released from the club as they had [[Todd Carney]], [[Braith Anasta]] and [[Mitchell Pearce]] able to fill positions in the halves.<ref name="smh april 2011">{{cite news|newspaper=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=Cooped-up Rooster gets a run with Tigers|author=Chris Barrett
|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-preview/coopedup-rooster-gets-a-run-with-tigers-20110331-1cnht.html|access-date=29 June 2011|date=1 April 2011}}</ref>

==Playing career==

=== Wests Tigers ===
Miller made his début with the Wests Tigers in round 4 of the 2011 NRL season, playing from the bench, and getting on the field with 20 minutes to go.<ref>{{cite news|newspaper=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=It's Miller's time at last as young cub earns stripes with late show|author=Glenn Jackson
|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/its-millers-time-at-last-as-young-cub-earns-stripes-with-late-show-20110403-1ctbk.html|access-date=29 June 2011|date=4 April 2011}}</ref> He was only 18 at the time, and coach [[Tim Sheens]] said, "If you're good enough, you're old enough. And I think he's good enough."<ref name="smh april 2011" /> Two weeks later he made his first appearance in the run-on squad, playing as halfback in a match against the [[Gold Coast Titans]],<ref>{{cite web|title=Custom Match List|url=http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|access-date=29 June 2011|publisher=RLP|archive-date=31 January 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170131030043/http://websites.sportstg.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|url-status=dead}}</ref> standing in for injured [[Robert Lui]].

Besides NRL appearances, Miller played in the Wests Tigers' [[2011 NRL Under-20s season|Toyota Cup]] team. He scored 5 tries and 128 points for the season, and was named as halfback in the competition's Team of the Year.<ref>{{cite news|publisher=SportsNewsFirst|title=Tiger cub wants key role|url=http://www.sportsnewsfirst.com.au/articles/2011/11/29/tiger-cub-wants-key-role/|access-date=29 November 2011|date=29 November 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120420230513/http://www.sportsnewsfirst.com.au/articles/2011/11/29/tiger-cub-wants-key-role/|archive-date=20 April 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In round 4 of the 2012 season, Miller, after a late call up, scored his first NRL try. However, it was to be his only first grade appearance for the year. He finished the year by playing a "starring role" as Wests Tigers won the [[2011 NRL Under-20s season|Under-20s]] grand final 46–6 against the [[Canberra Raiders]].<ref>{{cite news|author=Jon Tuxworth|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=Wighton to start in the halves for juniors|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/wighton-to-start-in-the-halves-for-juniors-20121010-27dtv.html|access-date=19 October 2012|date=11 October 2012}}</ref> He then kicked six goals as he played five-eighth in the [[Junior Kangaroos]] squad that beat New Zealand 48–16.<ref>{{cite web|author=Nathan Ross|work=weststigers.com.au|title=Miller happy with his year|url=http://www.weststigers.com.au/news-display/tigers/65117|access-date=19 October 2012|date=15 October 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121017235830/http://www.weststigers.com.au/news-display/tigers/65117|archive-date=17 October 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Beginning the 2013 season as the Wests Tigers first choice of halfback, Miller started in the first 4 games of the season before being dropped to reserve grade for [[Braith Anasta]].<ref>{{cite news|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|title=Braith Anasta has mixed emotions over move to halves for Wests Tigers|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/braith-anasta-excited-by-halfback-move/story-e6frexs9-1226613392094|access-date=14 May 2013|date=6 April 2013}}</ref>

=== Hull F.C. ===
Weeks later it was announced Miller had been released and signed for [[Hull F.C.]] on a {{frac|2|1|2}}-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|last=Holden|first=Matt|title=HULL END SEVEN SEARCH WITH AUSSIE STARLET MILLER|url=http://www.hullfc.com/article/25031/hull-end-seven-search-with-aussie-starlet-miller|work=hullfc.com|publisher=Hull FC|access-date=14 May 2013}}</ref>

It was announced Miller would leave the club at the end of the 2014 season.

=== Wakefield Trinity ===
He was subsequently signed by [[Wakefield Trinity]] for the 2015 season.<ref>{{cite web |date=21 November 2014 |title=Jacob Miller: Wakefield Trinity Wildcats sign ex-Hull FC half-back |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/30116471 |access-date=18 December 2014 |publisher=BBC Sport}}</ref> Miller played 200 matches for Wakefield Trinity over a seven-year period.

=== Castleford Tigers ===
In October 2022, Miller signed for the [[Castleford Tigers]], joining on a three-year deal from local rivals [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=4 October 2022 |title=Jacob Miller: Half-back moves from Wakefield to Castleford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63136105 |access-date=5 November 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He made his Castleford debut in round 1 against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]], scoring a try and being voted Fans Man of the Match.<ref>{{Cite web |date=19 February 2023 |title=Super League: Hull FC 32-30 Castleford Tigers - Away side fall just short of remarkable fightback |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64668525 |access-date=5 November 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{cite tweet|author=Castleford Tigers|author-link=Castleford Tigers|user=CTRLFC|number=1627639203185192960|title=Your first @HuntersHomes Fans Man of the Match of the 2023 season is @jmilky34!}}</ref> He scored a try and a drop goal against [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]] on 21 April,<ref>{{Cite web |date=21 April 2023 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 7-12 Hull KR - Rovers move within two points of leaders Warrington |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65322445 |access-date=5 November 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> and a further try against the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at the [[Magic Weekend#2023: Newcastle|Magic Weekend]] on 3 June.<ref>{{Cite web |date=3 June 2023 |title=Super League: Leeds Rhinos 24-26 Castleford - Tigers end six-game losing run |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65754053 |access-date=5 November 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> However, he also topped the league for missed tackles and registered only 1 try assist throughout the year.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Player stats - Jacob Miller |url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/player/176/jacob-miller |access-date=6 November 2023 |website=Super League}}</ref> He made a total of 26 appearances for Castleford in the 2023 season, as the Tigers narrowly avoided relegation by finishing above his previous club [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]].<ref>{{cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=22 September 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers ace Alex Mellor delivers brutally honest assessment of 'awful' season; 'We've massively underperformed' |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-ace-alex-mellor-delivers-brutally-honest-assessment-of-awful-season-weve-massively/ |access-date=6 November 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

Miller's strong performance in [[2024 Castleford Tigers season|2024 pre-season]] was highlighted by new head coach [[Craig Lingard]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chapman-Coombe |first=Louis |title=Exclusive: Castleford Tigers boss Craig Lingard on unlocking Jacob Miller and Innes Senior's arrival |url=https://www.casforum.co.uk/exclusive-castleford-tigers-boss-craig-lingard-on-unlocking-jacob-miller-and-innes-seniors-arrival-87145/ |access-date=24 March 2024 |website=casforum.co.uk}}</ref> In round 3, he was voted Fans Man of the Match against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]].<ref>{{cite tweet|author=Castleford Tigers|author-link=Castleford Tigers|user=CTRLFC|number=1764679065846350272|title=The @HuntersHomes Man of the Match from Friday night goes to... Jacob Miller!}}</ref> He scored his first try of the year against the [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[Challenge Cup]] on 23 March.<ref>{{Cite web |date=23 March 2024 |title=Challenge Cup: Batley Bulldogs 14-28 Castleford Tigers: Paul McShane stars as Super League side progress |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68616745 |access-date=24 March 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In July, he provided four try assists in a Man of the Match performance against [[London Broncos|London]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=12 July 2024 |title=Controlled Castleford beat bottom club Broncos |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c99wgzeeelwo |access-date=17 October 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{cite tweet|author=Betfred Super League|author-link=Super League|user=SuperLeague|number=1812804303616360902|title=Was this Jacob Miller's best performance in a @CTRLFC shirt? Four assists in the Round 17 win over the Broncos}}</ref> Towards the end of the season, Miller was occasionally rested as Lingard experimented with different halfback combinations,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Mitchell |first=Ollie |date=1 September 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers boss explains why he dropped a key man for Hull FC clash |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/castleford-tigers-boss-explains-why-he-dropped-a-key-man-for-hull-fc-clash-96463/ |access-date=17 October 2024 |website=Serious About Rugby League}}</ref> and he was linked with a move away from the club.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |date=1 October 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers closing in on half-back signing with Jacob Miller making way for overseas recruit |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-closing-in-on-half-back-signing-with-jacob-miller-making-way-for-overseas-recruit-4804018 |access-date=17 October 2024 |website=Wakefield Express}}</ref> On 7 October, it was announced that he would depart Castleford alongside eight teammates after being released from the final year of his contract.<ref>{{Cite web |date=7 October 2024 |title=Miller heads lists of nine Castleford departures |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c17lz77jrnqo |access-date=17 October 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=7 October 2024 |title=Jacob Miller heads Castleford Tigers list of departing players as club tease new signings |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/jacob-miller-heads-castleford-tigers-list-of-departing-players-as-club-tease-new-signings-4814169 |access-date=17 October 2024 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref>
